steps.title: Identify what the blank/question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. Map the passage's three actual moves: (1) a broad statement that millions of shipwrecks affect sea life and interest researchers, (2) a specific team's study of microbial communities near wooden wrecks in the Gulf of Mexico, (3) the study's key finding about peak microbial diversity at ~125 meters. The correct answer must match all three moves accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'introduces a study... summarizes results... describes a research team's reaction': The passage never describes the team's reaction to the study. The study is the team's own work, not something they reacted to. ✗
  • Choice B — 'notes a general scientific interest... describes a specific study... states one of the study's findings': Matches all three moves exactly: broad interest ("great interest to researchers"), specific study (Hamdan et al.), one finding (peak diversity at 125 m). ✓
  • Choice C — 'states the number of known shipwrecks, describes the historical significance of one... comments on microbes found there': The San Pedro is only mentioned by name in the opening sentence as an example of the millions of wrecks — its historical significance is never discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'names a famous historical shipwreck, describes the type of wood used to build that ship...': The passage uses pine and oak as sampling materials placed near wrecks, not as the construction material of any specific ship. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
